Welcoming the deity from {{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|阿須賀神社}} to the second hall of Kumano Hayatama Taisha. 2. A procession of the deity to the {{ill|Otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine). 3. Returning the deity to the second hall after rituals.
This structure does not merely represent a relocation from Asuka Shrine to Kumano Hayatama Taisha. According to Kumano legends, the deities first descended at {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社}}, were enshrined at a place north of Asuka Shrine, and then relocated to Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 166]</ref>.
Historically, the festival occurred on the 15th and 16th days of the ninth lunar month, a time associated with autumn harvest celebrations<ref>Sakurai [1988: 184]</ref>.
== Festival Schedule == The festival begins on October 14 with purification rituals for the sacred horse at Ohama Beach. Additional ceremonies include: - **Mame Kenji no Gi**: The horse is offered beans at Asuka Shrine. - **Kakeuo Kaya-ho Houken no Gi**: Offerings from Oshima in {{ill|Kushimoto Town|ja|串本町}}<ref>Ueno [1998: 20]</ref>.
=== Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15) === This ceremony honors Kumano Hayatamano Okami (enshrined in the second hall). Starting in the afternoon, a procession brings the deity from Asuka Shrine to the second hall. The procession continues with a large entourage leading the deity to the Otabisho<ref>Ueno [1998: 21]</ref>.
=== Ofune Festival (October 16) === Dedicated to Fusumi Okami (enshrined in the first hall), this festival features a grand procession of mikoshi and ceremonial boats on the Kumano River<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">Toyoshima [1992: 210]</ref>. The event concludes with ceremonial rowing and rituals at Ofunejima Island<ref name="Ueno_1998_22">Ueno [1998: 22]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == - **Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals**: National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (2016)<ref name="NationalDesignation"/>. - **Gilded Portable Shrine**: Donated by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, designated as a National Important Cultural Property in 1897<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|title=Gilded Portable Shrine|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>. - **Gilded Sacred Boat**: Also a National Important Cultural Property (1897)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|title=Gilded Sacred Boat|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>.

{{Image request|images showing the festival, especially sacred horses, mikoshi, and ships|date=September 2009|cat=Shingu City|cat2=October}} '''Kumano Hayatama Festival''' (熊野速玉祭, Kumano Hayatama Matsuri) is the {{ill|annual festival|ja|例大祭}} of the {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} in {{ill|Shingu City|ja|新宮市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It takes place every year from October 15 to 16 and is best known for the '''Shinme Togyo Ceremony''' (神馬渡御式) on October 15 and the '''Ofune Festival''' (御船祭) on October 16.
The festival has been designated a {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government (March 2, 2016, together with the {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭}})<ref name="NationalDesignation">{{National Database of Cultural Properties 2|name=Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals|register=302|item=00000934|year=2016|jyear=平成28|month=3|date=2|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|class=Customs and Rituals, Festivals (Faith)|accessdate=July 31, 2016}}</ref>.
== Festival of Kumano Gongen's Arrival == The Kumano Hayatama Taisha enshrines multiple deities collectively known as the "Twelve Deities of Kumano." However, the main festival focuses on the principal deities, {{ill|Kumano Hayatamano Okami|ja|熊野速玉男神}} and {{ill|Fusumi Okami|ja|夫須美大神}}. According to religious and folklore studies, the festival reenacts the journey of divine spirits ascending the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}}, settling on {{ill|Ofunejima Island|ja|御船島}}, and then moving through Otsumoto River to the shrine in Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 172]</ref>.
On October 15, the {{ill|Shinme Togyo Ceremony|ja|神馬渡御式}} (see [[#Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15)|below]]) involves: 1. Welcoming the deity from {{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|阿須賀神社}} to the second hall of Kumano Hayatama Taisha. 2. A procession of the deity to the {{ill|Otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine). 3. Returning the deity to the second hall after rituals.
This structure does not merely represent a relocation from Asuka Shrine to Kumano Hayatama Taisha. According to Kumano legends, the deities first descended at {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社}}, were enshrined at a place north of Asuka Shrine, and then relocated to Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 166]</ref>.
Historically, the festival occurred on the 15th and 16th days of the ninth lunar month, a time associated with autumn harvest celebrations<ref>Sakurai [1988: 184]</ref>.
== Festival Schedule == The festival begins on October 14 with purification rituals for the sacred horse at Ohama Beach. Additional ceremonies include: - **Mame Kenji no Gi**: The horse is offered beans at Asuka Shrine. - **Kakeuo Kaya-ho Houken no Gi**: Offerings from Oshima in {{ill|Kushimoto Town|ja|串本町}}<ref>Ueno [1998: 20]</ref>.
=== Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15) === This ceremony honors Kumano Hayatamano Okami (enshrined in the second hall). Starting in the afternoon, a procession brings the deity from Asuka Shrine to the second hall. The procession continues with a large entourage leading the deity to the Otabisho<ref>Ueno [1998: 21]</ref>.
=== Ofune Festival (October 16) === Dedicated to Fusumi Okami (enshrined in the first hall), this festival features a grand procession of mikoshi and ceremonial boats on the Kumano River<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">Toyoshima [1992: 210]</ref>. The event concludes with ceremonial rowing and rituals at Ofunejima Island<ref name="Ueno_1998_22">Ueno [1998: 22]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == - **Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals**: National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (2016)<ref name="NationalDesignation"/>. - **Gilded Portable Shrine**: Donated by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, designated as a National Important Cultural Property in 1897<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|title=Gilded Portable Shrine|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>. - **Gilded Sacred Boat**: Also a National Important Cultural Property (1897)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|title=Gilded Sacred Boat|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>.

"Festivals of the Three Mountains." In Wada, Atsushi (ed.), *Kumano Gongen*. Tokyo: Chikuma Shobo. * Toyoshima, Osamu. 1992. *Land of the Dead, Kumano - The Sacred Faith of the Japanese People.* Tokyo: Kodansha.

{{Image request|images showing the festival, especially sacred horses, mikoshi, and ships|date=September 2009|cat=Shingu City|cat2=October}} '''Kumano Hayatama Festival''' (熊野速玉祭, Kumano Hayatama Matsuri) is the {{ill|annual festival|ja|例大祭}} of the {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} in {{ill|Shingu City|ja|新宮市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It takes place every year from October 15 to 16 and is best known for the '''Shinme Togyo Ceremony''' (神馬渡御式) on October 15 and the '''Ofune Festival''' (御船祭) on October 16.
The festival has been designated a {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government (March 2, 2016, together with the {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭}})<ref name="NationalDesignation">{{National Database of Cultural Properties 2|name=Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals|register=302|item=00000934|year=2016|jyear=平成28|month=3|date=2|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|class=Customs and Rituals, Festivals (Faith)|accessdate=July 31, 2016}}</ref>.
== Festival of Kumano Gongen's Arrival == The Kumano Hayatama Taisha enshrines multiple deities collectively known as the "Twelve Deities of Kumano." However, the main festival focuses on the principal deities, {{ill|Kumano Hayatamano Okami|ja|熊野速玉男神}} and {{ill|Fusumi Okami|ja|夫須美大神}}. According to religious and folklore studies, the festival reenacts the journey of divine spirits ascending the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川}}, settling on {{ill|Ofunejima Island|ja|御船島}}, and then moving through Otsumoto River to the shrine in Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 172]</ref>.
On October 15, the {{ill|Shinme Togyo Ceremony|ja|神馬渡御式}} (see [[#Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15)|below]]) involves: 1. Welcoming the deity from {{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|阿須賀神社}} to the second hall of Kumano Hayatama Taisha. 2. A procession of the deity to the {{ill|Otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine). 3. Returning the deity to the second hall after rituals.
This structure does not merely represent a relocation from Asuka Shrine to Kumano Hayatama Taisha. According to Kumano legends, the deities first descended at {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社}}, were enshrined at a place north of Asuka Shrine, and then relocated to Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 166]</ref>.
Historically, the festival occurred on the 15th and 16th days of the ninth lunar month, a time associated with autumn harvest celebrations<ref>Sakurai [1988: 184]</ref>.
== Festival Schedule == The festival begins on October 14 with purification rituals for the sacred horse at Ohama Beach. Additional ceremonies include: - **Mame Kenji no Gi**: The horse is offered beans at Asuka Shrine. - **Kakeuo Kaya-ho Houken no Gi**: Offerings from Oshima in {{ill|Kushimoto Town|ja|串本町}}<ref>Ueno [1998: 20]</ref>.
=== Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15) === This ceremony honors Kumano Hayatamano Okami (enshrined in the second hall). Starting in the afternoon, a procession brings the deity from Asuka Shrine to the second hall. The procession continues with a large entourage leading the deity to the Otabisho<ref>Ueno [1998: 21]</ref>.
=== Ofune Festival (October 16) === Dedicated to Fusumi Okami (enshrined in the first hall), this festival features a grand procession of mikoshi and ceremonial boats on the Kumano River<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">Toyoshima [1992: 210]</ref>. The event concludes with ceremonial rowing and rituals at Ofunejima Island<ref name="Ueno_1998_22">Ueno [1998: 22]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == - **Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals**: National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (2016)<ref name="NationalDesignation"/>. - **Gilded Portable Shrine**: Donated by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, designated as a National Important Cultural Property in 1897<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|title=Gilded Portable Shrine|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>. - **Gilded Sacred Boat**: Also a National Important Cultural Property (1897)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|title=Gilded Sacred Boat|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>.
== Notes == {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * Ueno, Motoharu. 1998. "Shinme Togyo Ceremony and Ofune Festival." In Kato, Takahisa (ed.), *Kumano Sanzan Faith Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Ebisukosyo Shuppan. * Sakurai, Mitsuru. 1988. "Festivals of the Three Mountains." In Wada, Atsushi (ed.), *Kumano Gongen*. Tokyo: Chikuma Shobo. * Toyoshima, Osamu. 1992. *Land of the Dead, Kumano - The Sacred Faith of the Japanese People.* Tokyo: Kodansha.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭}}: The grand fire festival held at the subsidiary {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社}}.

== Festival of Kumano Gongen's Arrival == The Kumano Hayatama Taisha enshrines multiple deities collectively known as the "Twelve Deities of Kumano." However, the main festival focuses on the principal deities, {{ill|Kumano Hayatamano Okami|ja|熊野速玉男神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Fusumi Okami|ja|夫須美大神|12=draft}}. According to religious and folklore studies, the festival reenacts the journey of divine spirits ascending the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|12=draft}}, settling on {{ill|Ofunejima Island|ja|御船島|12=draft}}, and then moving through Otsumoto River to the shrine in Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 172]</ref>.
On October 15, the {{ill|Shinme Togyo Ceremony|ja|神馬渡御式|12=draft}} (see [[#Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15)|below]]) involves: 1. Welcoming the deity from {{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|阿須賀神社|12=draft}} to the second hall of Kumano Hayatama Taisha. 2. A procession of the deity to the {{ill|Otabisho|ja|御旅所|12=draft}} (temporary shrine). 3. Returning the deity to the second hall after rituals.
This structure does not merely represent a relocation from Asuka Shrine to Kumano Hayatama Taisha. According to Kumano legends, the deities first descended at {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|12=draft}}, were enshrined at a place north of Asuka Shrine, and then relocated to Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 166]</ref>.
Historically, the festival occurred on the 15th and 16th days of the ninth lunar month, a time associated with autumn harvest celebrations<ref>Sakurai [1988: 184]</ref>.
== Festival Schedule == The festival begins on October 14 with purification rituals for the sacred horse at Ohama Beach. Additional ceremonies include: - **Mame Kenji no Gi**: The horse is offered beans at Asuka Shrine. - **Kakeuo Kaya-ho Houken no Gi**: Offerings from Oshima in {{ill|Kushimoto Town|ja|串本町|12=draft}}<ref>Ueno [1998: 20]</ref>.
=== Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15) === This ceremony honors Kumano Hayatamano Okami (enshrined in the second hall). Starting in the afternoon, a procession brings the deity from Asuka Shrine to the second hall. The procession continues with a large entourage leading the deity to the Otabisho<ref>Ueno [1998: 21]</ref>.
=== Ofune Festival (October 16) === Dedicated to Fusumi Okami (enshrined in the first hall), this festival features a grand procession of mikoshi and ceremonial boats on the Kumano River<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">Toyoshima [1992: 210]</ref>. The event concludes with ceremonial rowing and rituals at Ofunejima Island<ref name="Ueno_1998_22">Ueno [1998: 22]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == - **Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals**: National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (2016)<ref name="NationalDesignation"/>. - **Gilded Portable Shrine**: Donated by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, designated as a National Important Cultural Property in 1897<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|title=Gilded Portable Shrine|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>. - **Gilded Sacred Boat**: Also a National Important Cultural Property (1897)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|title=Gilded Sacred Boat|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>.

"Festivals of the Three Mountains." In Wada, Atsushi (ed.), *Kumano Gongen*. Tokyo: Chikuma Shobo. * Toyoshima, Osamu. 1992. *Land of the Dead, Kumano - The Sacred Faith of the Japanese People.* Tokyo: Kodansha.

The procession continues with a large entourage leading the deity to the Otabisho<ref>Ueno [1998: 21]</ref>.
=== Ofune Festival (October 16) === Dedicated to Fusumi Okami (enshrined in the first hall), this festival features a grand procession of mikoshi and ceremonial boats on the Kumano River<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">Toyoshima [1992: 210]</ref>. The event concludes with ceremonial rowing and rituals at Ofunejima Island<ref name="Ueno_1998_22">Ueno [1998: 22]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == - **Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals**: National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (2016)<ref name="NationalDesignation"/>. - **Gilded Portable Shrine**: Donated by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, designated as a National Important Cultural Property in 1897<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|title=Gilded Portable Shrine|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>. - **Gilded Sacred Boat**: Also a National Important Cultural Property (1897)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|title=Gilded Sacred Boat|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>.
== Notes == {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * Ueno, Motoharu. 1998. "Shinme Togyo Ceremony and Ofune Festival." In Kato, Takahisa (ed.), *Kumano Sanzan Faith Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Ebisukosyo Shuppan. * Sakurai, Mitsuru. 1988. "Festivals of the Three Mountains." In Wada, Atsushi (ed.), *Kumano Gongen*. Tokyo: Chikuma Shobo. * Toyoshima, Osamu. 1992. *Land of the Dead, Kumano - The Sacred Faith of the Japanese People.* Tokyo: Kodansha.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭|12=draft}}: The grand fire festival held at the subsidiary {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|12=draft}}.

{{Image request|images showing the festival, especially sacred horses, mikoshi, and ships|date=September 2009|cat=Shingu City|cat2=October}} '''Kumano Hayatama Festival''' (熊野速玉祭, Kumano Hayatama Matsuri) is the {{ill|annual festival|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shingu City|ja|新宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}. It takes place every year from October 15 to 16 and is best known for the '''Shinme Togyo Ceremony''' (神馬渡御式) on October 15 and the '''Ofune Festival''' (御船祭) on October 16.
The festival has been designated a {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} by the Japanese government (March 2, 2016, together with the {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭|12=draft}})<ref name="NationalDesignation">{{National Database of Cultural Properties 2|name=Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals|register=302|item=00000934|year=2016|jyear=平成28|month=3|date=2|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|class=Customs and Rituals, Festivals (Faith)|accessdate=July 31, 2016}}</ref>.
== Festival of Kumano Gongen's Arrival == The Kumano Hayatama Taisha enshrines multiple deities collectively known as the "Twelve Deities of Kumano." However, the main festival focuses on the principal deities, {{ill|Kumano Hayatamano Okami|ja|熊野速玉男神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Fusumi Okami|ja|夫須美大神|12=draft}}. According to religious and folklore studies, the festival reenacts the journey of divine spirits ascending the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|12=draft}}, settling on {{ill|Ofunejima Island|ja|御船島|12=draft}}, and then moving through Otsumoto River to the shrine in Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 172]</ref>.
On October 15, the {{ill|Shinme Togyo Ceremony|ja|神馬渡御式|12=draft}} (see [[#Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15)|below]]) involves: 1. Welcoming the deity from {{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|阿須賀神社|12=draft}} to the second hall of Kumano Hayatama Taisha. 2. A procession of the deity to the {{ill|Otabisho|ja|御旅所|12=draft}} (temporary shrine). 3. Returning the deity to the second hall after rituals.
This structure does not merely represent a relocation from Asuka Shrine to Kumano Hayatama Taisha. According to Kumano legends, the deities first descended at {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|12=draft}}, were enshrined at a place north of Asuka Shrine, and then relocated to Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 166]</ref>.
Historically, the festival occurred on the 15th and 16th days of the ninth lunar month, a time associated with autumn harvest celebrations<ref>Sakurai [1988: 184]</ref>.
== Festival Schedule == The festival begins on October 14 with purification rituals for the sacred horse at Ohama Beach. Additional ceremonies include: - **Mame Kenji no Gi**: The horse is offered beans at Asuka Shrine. - **Kakeuo Kaya-ho Houken no Gi**: Offerings from Oshima in {{ill|Kushimoto Town|ja|串本町|12=draft}}<ref>Ueno [1998: 20]</ref>.
=== Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15) === This ceremony honors Kumano Hayatamano Okami (enshrined in the second hall). Starting in the afternoon, a procession brings the deity from Asuka Shrine to the second hall. The procession continues with a large entourage leading the deity to the Otabisho<ref>Ueno [1998: 21]</ref>.
=== Ofune Festival (October 16) === Dedicated to Fusumi Okami (enshrined in the first hall), this festival features a grand procession of mikoshi and ceremonial boats on the Kumano River<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">Toyoshima [1992: 210]</ref>. The event concludes with ceremonial rowing and rituals at Ofunejima Island<ref name="Ueno_1998_22">Ueno [1998: 22]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == - **Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals**: National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (2016)<ref name="NationalDesignation"/>. - **Gilded Portable Shrine**: Donated by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, designated as a National Important Cultural Property in 1897<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|title=Gilded Portable Shrine|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>. - **Gilded Sacred Boat**: Also a National Important Cultural Property (1897)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|title=Gilded Sacred Boat|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>.
== Notes == {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * Ueno, Motoharu. 1998. "Shinme Togyo Ceremony and Ofune Festival." In Kato, Takahisa (ed.), *Kumano Sanzan Faith Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Ebisukosyo Shuppan. * Sakurai, Mitsuru. 1988. "Festivals of the Three Mountains." In Wada, Atsushi (ed.), *Kumano Gongen*. Tokyo: Chikuma Shobo. * Toyoshima, Osamu. 1992. *Land of the Dead, Kumano - The Sacred Faith of the Japanese People.* Tokyo: Kodansha.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭|12=draft}}: The grand fire festival held at the subsidiary {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|12=draft}}.

revision.comment: 34 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:熊野速玉祭 up to rev 98189752
revision.text:
{{画像提供依頼|祭礼の様子が分かる画像、特に神馬、神輿、御船|date=2009年9月|cat=新宮市|cat2=10月}} '''熊野速玉祭'''（くまのはやたままつり）は[[熊野速玉大社]]（[[和歌山県]][[新宮市]]）の例大祭。例年10月15日から16日に執行され、15日の'''神馬渡御式'''（しんめとぎょしき）、16日の'''御船祭'''（みふねまつり）で知られる。
国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている（[[2016年]]〈[[平成]]28年〉3月2日指定、[[御燈祭]]と合わせて）<ref name="国指定">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name = 新宮の速玉祭・御燈祭り|register = 302|item = 00000934|year = 2016|jyear = 平成28|month = 3|date = 2|cat1 = 重要無形民俗文化財|class = 風俗慣習、祭礼（信仰）|other = |accessdate = 2016-07-31}}、平成28年3月2日文部科学省告示第40号</ref>。
== 熊野権現来臨の祭 == 速玉大社は「熊野十二所権現」と総称される複数の神を祀るが、本祭礼はその中でも主祭神である[[熊野速玉男神|速玉大神]]と夫須美大神に対してのもので、宗教民俗学的な解釈に従えば、常世から来た神霊が[[熊野川]]を遡上して[[御船島|御舟島]]に鎮座した後、乙基河原をへて新宮に遷座したという熊野権現来臨のありさまを再現した祭りである<ref>桜井[1988: 172]</ref>。
10月15日の神馬渡御式（[[#神馬渡御式（10月15日）|後述]]）は、 # [[阿須賀神社]]の神霊を速玉大社第二殿に迎える # 神霊が御旅所へ渡御する # 御旅所での祭儀の後、神霊が第二殿に還御する という構造をとっている。しかしながら、この構造を単に阿須賀神社から速玉大社への遷座と見ることはできない<ref>桜井[1988: 167]</ref>。「熊野権現御垂迹縁起」（『[[長寛勘文]]』所収）等に見られる熊野権現の縁起譚が伝えるところによれば、熊野権現は熊野の地において熊野権現はまず[[神倉神社]]に降臨し、次に阿須賀神社北側にある石淵（いわぶち）谷<ref> 今日の[[貴禰谷神社]]（[[三重県]][[紀宝町]]）と考えられている[平凡社 1997: 221]。</ref>に[[勧請]]されて、その時に初めて結早玉・家津美御子と称した。その後、石淵谷から新宮に遷座した<ref>桜井[1988: 166]</ref>としており、阿須賀から新宮にではない。すなわち、神馬渡御式の構造とは、熊野権現が石淵谷から新宮へと遷座した過程を表しているのである<ref>桜井[1988: 168]</ref>。
また明治以前まで祭が開かれていた旧暦9月15日から16日は、秋の末の望の日であり、近世の記録によれば14日には[[田楽]]も行われていたという<ref>桜井[1998: 173]</ref>。こうした点から、速玉祭は秋の収穫祭としての性格を帯びている<ref>桜井[1998: 184]</ref>。
== 祭礼次第 == 祭礼は前日の10月14日から始まる。この日には、神馬を大浜海岸に連れて行き、潮浴びで体を清めた後、旧摂社阿須賀神社にて豆を食う「豆献ジノ儀（まめけんじのぎ）」と、[[串本町]]大島地区からの供物の奉献を受ける「掛魚萱穂奉献ノ儀（かけうおかやほほうけんのぎ）」の2つの儀式が行われる<ref>上野[1998: 20]</ref>。大島地区から奉献された供物のうち、掛魚は15日の神馬渡御式で、萱穂は16日の御船祭で使われるものである<ref>上野[1998: 21-22]</ref>。
=== 神馬渡御式（10月15日） === 速玉大神（第二殿に鎮座）に対しての祭典。10月15日の午後より、宮司以下の一行が阿須賀神社へ行列で向かい、拝殿内で唐鞍を置いて神馬を飾りつけ、神霊を迎える。神霊を乗せた神馬が大社に戻ると、神霊を大社第二殿に移し、祝詞・神楽を奏上して神幸祭を執り行う。その後、宮司が神馬に再び神霊を迎え、警固、楽人、神楽人、神官、随員ほか200名にも達する行列を引きつれ、熊野川の河原を進み、[[千穂ヶ峯|権現山]]西北にある杉ノ仮宮（御旅所）へ渡御する。宮司は神霊を仮宮に奉遷し、松明の明かりに照らされながら神楽・祝詞を奏上し、掛魚、神酒、神饌を供え、儀式の終了後は、夜闇の中、神霊を奉じて大社に戻る<ref>以上、上野[1998: 21]</ref>。
=== 御船祭（10月16日） === [[File:御船祭.JPG|thumb|280px|日も落ちる頃、速玉大社裏手の河原へ戻る早船（2014年）。]] [[File:御船祭 早船.JPG|thumb|280px|早船の競漕が終わった後でも勇壮に船を漕ぎ続ける（2014年）。]] 夫須美大神（第一殿に鎮座）に対しての祭典。神馬渡御式の翌10月16日朝8時から祭員が神輿・神幸船・ヒトツモノなどの祭具の準備をすすめる<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">豊島[1992: 210]</ref>。熊野速玉大社には、[[足利義満]]奉納のもので重要文化財に指定された神輿が所蔵されているが、祭礼で用いられるのは代りの品である<ref name="Sakurai_1988_169">桜井[1988: 169]</ref>。神輿は準備が終わり次第、第一殿前に置かれる。神幸船は、全体が朱の漆塗り、黒漆塗りの唐棟造の御座殿のもので、重要文化財である<ref name="Sakurai_1988_169"/>。ヒトツモノとは、神馬に載せる人形で、金襴の狩衣をまとい、12本の萱穂と12枚の[[熊野牛王符|牛王宝印]]を腰にさし、編み笠をかぶり、熊野権現の神霊の憑坐（よりまし）と解されるものである<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210"/><ref name="Sakurai_1988_170">桜井[1988: 170]</ref>。
午後からは、第一殿にて祭典が執り行われ、宮司が神霊を神輿に移す。御旗に続いてヒトツモノが祭の行列を先導し、熊野川の河原へ出る<ref name="Sakurai_1988_170"/>。覆面をした宮司が神霊を神輿から神幸船に移し、宮司ら神職と楽人が斎主船に乗り込む。神幸船と斎主船をさらに諸手船（もろたぶね）が曳航し、これらをさらに9艘の早船が先導する。諸手船の舵をとるのは、熊野川河口の鵜殿（三重県紀宝町）の住人である<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212">豊島[1992: 212]</ref>。これは、石淵谷から新宮への熊野権現の遷座に際して、鵜殿の住人が船で先導したとの伝承にもとづくものである<ref>平凡社[1983]</ref>。また、諸手船には、ハリワイセと呼ばれる、赤衣をまとった女装の男性が櫂をもって舷に立っている<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212"/><ref name="Ueno_1998_22">上野[1998: 22]</ref>。
御舟島の下流1キロメートルにある牛の鼻という急流を過ぎたところで、早船の競漕が始まる。早船は御舟島を右回りに3周して勝敗を競い、乙基（おとも）河原に着岸する。その後、諸手船以下の3艘が御舟島を2回ゆっくりと回る。このとき、ハリワイセは、「ハリハリセー」と数回唱えながら、片手をかざして遠望する姿をとる「ハリハリ踊り」を演じる<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212"/><ref name="Ueno_1998_22"/>。次いで、御舟島から使者が扇子で3回招くと、9艘の早船が再び競漕を始める。今度は、御舟島を左から2周回って、大社裏手の川岸に向かう。
他方で神幸船と斎主船は乙基河原に上陸する。神輿に遷された神霊とともに神職ら一行は御旅所へ渡御し、前夜と同様の祭儀を執行した後、やはり夜闇の中、神霊を奉じて大社に戻り<ref name="Ueno_1998_22"/>、神霊を第二殿におさめて<ref>豊島[1992:212-213]</ref>、祭りは終了する。
== 文化財 == * 新宮の速玉祭・御燈祭り - 国の重要無形民俗文化財（2016年〈平成28年〉3月2日指定、[[御燈祭]]と合わせて）<ref name="国指定"/> ** 以前は和歌山県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]であったが（[[1964年]]〈[[昭和]]39年〉5月28日指定）<ref name="Bunkazai">{{Cite web|和書 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenminzoku.html|title=県指定文化財・民俗文化財|accessdate=2009-09-23}}</ref>、国指定にともない県の文化財としての指定は解除された<ref>[https://b2b-ch.infomart.co.jp/news/detail.page;JSESSIONID_B2BCH=61b050ab700812bab2d13b7a977a?0&IMNEWS1=914519 和歌山県指定文化財の新規指定等について]</ref>。<!--国の指定にともなう県指定の解除は本件に限った話ではなく、特記の必要なしか?--> * きゅう漆金銅装神輿（きゅうしつこんどうそうしんよ、「きゅう」は「かみがしら」に「休」） - 足利義満奉献の品と伝わる。室町時代。熊野速玉大社蔵。国の[[重要文化財]]（美術工芸品、[[1897年]]〈[[明治]]30年〉12月28日指定）<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425 |title=きゅう漆金銅 装神輿 |publisher= 文化庁 |work= 国指定文化財等 データベース |accessdate=2010-06-12 }}</ref>。 * きゅう漆金銅装神幸用船（きゅうしつこんどうそうしんこうようふね、「きゅう」は「かみがしら」に「休」） - 江戸時代。熊野速玉大社蔵。国の重要文化財（美術工芸品、1897年〈[[明治]]30年〉12月28日指定）<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426 |title=きゅう漆金銅装神幸用船 |publisher=文化庁 |work= 国指定文化財等 データベース |accessdate=2010-06-12 }}</ref>。
== 注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 文献 == * 上野 元、1998、「神馬渡御式と御船祭」、加藤 隆久編、『熊野三山信仰事典』、戎光祥出版 ISBN 4900901075 * 桜井 満、1988、「三山の祭り」、和田 萃編、『熊野権現』、筑摩書房 ISBN 4480854215 * 全日本郷土芸能協会編、2006、『日本の祭り文化事典』、東京書籍 ISBN 4487733332 * 豊島 修、1992、『死の国・熊野 - 日本人の聖地信仰』、講談社（講談社現代新書） ISBN 4061491032 * 平凡社編、1983、『三重県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系24）、平凡社 ISBN 9784582490244 * — 、1997、『大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典』、平凡社 ISBN 4582134025
== 関連項目 == * [[御燈祭]] - 末社[[神倉神社]]の例大祭。勇壮な火祭りとして知られる。
<!--リンク切れ== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kumanokaido.com/hayatama/index.htm 熊野速玉大社] - 熊野街道.COM内。祭礼の動画・写真を公開。 * [http://www.kumanokaido.com/hayatama/index.htm 熊野速玉大社 行事・祭礼] - 神社公式。--> {{熊野三山の祭り}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くまのはやたままつり}} [[Category:熊野三山|祭くまのはやたま]] [[Category:神道行事]] [[Category:重要無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の祭り]] [[Category:新宮市の文化]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[熊野速玉祭]] to [[Kumano Hayatama Festival]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{画像提供依頼|祭礼の様子が分かる画像、特に神馬、神輿、御船|date=2009年9月|cat=新宮市|cat2=10月}} '''熊野速玉祭'''（くまのはやたままつり）は[[熊野速玉大社]]（[[和歌山県]][[新宮市]]）の例大祭。例年10月15日から16日に執行され、15日の'''神馬渡御式'''（しんめとぎょしき）、16日の'''御船祭'''（みふねまつり）で知られる。
国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている（[[2016年]]〈[[平成]]28年〉3月2日指定、[[御燈祭]]と合わせて）<ref name="国指定">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name = 新宮の速玉祭・御燈祭り|register = 302|item = 00000934|year = 2016|jyear = 平成28|month = 3|date = 2|cat1 = 重要無形民俗文化財|class = 風俗慣習、祭礼（信仰）|other = |accessdate = 2016-07-31}}、平成28年3月2日文部科学省告示第40号</ref>。
== 熊野権現来臨の祭 == 速玉大社は「熊野十二所権現」と総称される複数の神を祀るが、本祭礼はその中でも主祭神である[[熊野速玉男神|速玉大神]]と夫須美大神に対してのもので、宗教民俗学的な解釈に従えば、常世から来た神霊が[[熊野川]]を遡上して[[御船島|御舟島]]に鎮座した後、乙基河原をへて新宮に遷座したという熊野権現来臨のありさまを再現した祭りである<ref>桜井[1988: 172]</ref>。
10月15日の神馬渡御式（[[#神馬渡御式（10月15日）|後述]]）は、 # [[阿須賀神社]]の神霊を速玉大社第二殿に迎える # 神霊が御旅所へ渡御する # 御旅所での祭儀の後、神霊が第二殿に還御する という構造をとっている。しかしながら、この構造を単に阿須賀神社から速玉大社への遷座と見ることはできない<ref>桜井[1988: 167]</ref>。「熊野権現御垂迹縁起」（『[[長寛勘文]]』所収）等に見られる熊野権現の縁起譚が伝えるところによれば、熊野権現は熊野の地において熊野権現はまず[[神倉神社]]に降臨し、次に阿須賀神社北側にある石淵（いわぶち）谷<ref> 今日の[[貴禰谷神社]]（[[三重県]][[紀宝町]]）と考えられている[平凡社 1997: 221]。</ref>に[[勧請]]されて、その時に初めて結早玉・家津美御子と称した。その後、石淵谷から新宮に遷座した<ref>桜井[1988: 166]</ref>としており、阿須賀から新宮にではない。すなわち、神馬渡御式の構造とは、熊野権現が石淵谷から新宮へと遷座した過程を表しているのである<ref>桜井[1988: 168]</ref>。
また明治以前まで祭が開かれていた旧暦9月15日から16日は、秋の末の望の日であり、近世の記録によれば14日には[[田楽]]も行われていたという<ref>桜井[1998: 173]</ref>。こうした点から、速玉祭は秋の収穫祭としての性格を帯びている<ref>桜井[1998: 184]</ref>。
== 祭礼次第 == 祭礼は前日の10月14日から始まる。この日には、神馬を大浜海岸に連れて行き、潮浴びで体を清めた後、旧摂社阿須賀神社にて豆を食う「豆献ジノ儀（まめけんじのぎ）」と、[[串本町]]大島地区からの供物の奉献を受ける「掛魚萱穂奉献ノ儀（かけうおかやほほうけんのぎ）」の2つの儀式が行われる<ref>上野[1998: 20]</ref>。大島地区から奉献された供物のうち、掛魚は15日の神馬渡御式で、萱穂は16日の御船祭で使われるものである<ref>上野[1998: 21-22]</ref>。
=== 神馬渡御式（10月15日） === 速玉大神（第二殿に鎮座）に対しての祭典。10月15日の午後より、宮司以下の一行が阿須賀神社へ行列で向かい、拝殿内で唐鞍を置いて神馬を飾りつけ、神霊を迎える。神霊を乗せた神馬が大社に戻ると、神霊を大社第二殿に移し、祝詞・神楽を奏上して神幸祭を執り行う。その後、宮司が神馬に再び神霊を迎え、警固、楽人、神楽人、神官、随員ほか200名にも達する行列を引きつれ、熊野川の河原を進み、[[千穂ヶ峯|権現山]]西北にある杉ノ仮宮（御旅所）へ渡御する。宮司は神霊を仮宮に奉遷し、松明の明かりに照らされながら神楽・祝詞を奏上し、掛魚、神酒、神饌を供え、儀式の終了後は、夜闇の中、神霊を奉じて大社に戻る<ref>以上、上野[1998: 21]</ref>。
=== 御船祭（10月16日） === [[File:御船祭.JPG|thumb|280px|日も落ちる頃、速玉大社裏手の河原へ戻る早船（2014年）。]] [[File:御船祭 早船.JPG|thumb|280px|早船の競漕が終わった後でも勇壮に船を漕ぎ続ける（2014年）。]] 夫須美大神（第一殿に鎮座）に対しての祭典。神馬渡御式の翌10月16日朝8時から祭員が神輿・神幸船・ヒトツモノなどの祭具の準備をすすめる<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">豊島[1992: 210]</ref>。熊野速玉大社には、[[足利義満]]奉納のもので重要文化財に指定された神輿が所蔵されているが、祭礼で用いられるのは代りの品である<ref name="Sakurai_1988_169">桜井[1988: 169]</ref>。神輿は準備が終わり次第、第一殿前に置かれる。神幸船は、全体が朱の漆塗り、黒漆塗りの唐棟造の御座殿のもので、重要文化財である<ref name="Sakurai_1988_169"/>。ヒトツモノとは、神馬に載せる人形で、金襴の狩衣をまとい、12本の萱穂と12枚の[[熊野牛王符|牛王宝印]]を腰にさし、編み笠をかぶり、熊野権現の神霊の憑坐（よりまし）と解されるものである<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210"/><ref name="Sakurai_1988_170">桜井[1988: 170]</ref>。
午後からは、第一殿にて祭典が執り行われ、宮司が神霊を神輿に移す。御旗に続いてヒトツモノが祭の行列を先導し、熊野川の河原へ出る<ref name="Sakurai_1988_170"/>。覆面をした宮司が神霊を神輿から神幸船に移し、宮司ら神職と楽人が斎主船に乗り込む。神幸船と斎主船をさらに諸手船（もろたぶね）が曳航し、これらをさらに9艘の早船が先導する。諸手船の舵をとるのは、熊野川河口の鵜殿（三重県紀宝町）の住人である<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212">豊島[1992: 212]</ref>。これは、石淵谷から新宮への熊野権現の遷座に際して、鵜殿の住人が船で先導したとの伝承にもとづくものである<ref>平凡社[1983]</ref>。また、諸手船には、ハリワイセと呼ばれる、赤衣をまとった女装の男性が櫂をもって舷に立っている<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212"/><ref name="Ueno_1998_22">上野[1998: 22]</ref>。
御舟島の下流1キロメートルにある牛の鼻という急流を過ぎたところで、早船の競漕が始まる。早船は御舟島を右回りに3周して勝敗を競い、乙基（おとも）河原に着岸する。その後、諸手船以下の3艘が御舟島を2回ゆっくりと回る。このとき、ハリワイセは、「ハリハリセー」と数回唱えながら、片手をかざして遠望する姿をとる「ハリハリ踊り」を演じる<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212"/><ref name="Ueno_1998_22"/>。次いで、御舟島から使者が扇子で3回招くと、9艘の早船が再び競漕を始める。今度は、御舟島を左から2周回って、大社裏手の川岸に向かう。
他方で神幸船と斎主船は乙基河原に上陸する。神輿に遷された神霊とともに神職ら一行は御旅所へ渡御し、前夜と同様の祭儀を執行した後、やはり夜闇の中、神霊を奉じて大社に戻り<ref name="Ueno_1998_22"/>、神霊を第二殿におさめて<ref>豊島[1992:212-213]</ref>、祭りは終了する。
== 文化財 == * 新宮の速玉祭・御燈祭り - 国の重要無形民俗文化財（2016年〈平成28年〉3月2日指定、[[御燈祭]]と合わせて）<ref name="国指定"/> ** 以前は和歌山県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]であったが（[[1964年]]〈[[昭和]]39年〉5月28日指定）<ref name="Bunkazai">{{Cite web|和書 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenminzoku.html|title=県指定文化財・民俗文化財|accessdate=2009-09-23}}</ref>、国指定にともない県の文化財としての指定は解除された<ref>[https://b2b-ch.infomart.co.jp/news/detail.page;JSESSIONID_B2BCH=61b050ab700812bab2d13b7a977a?0&IMNEWS1=914519 和歌山県指定文化財の新規指定等について]</ref>。<!--国の指定にともなう県指定の解除は本件に限った話ではなく、特記の必要なしか?--> * きゅう漆金銅装神輿（きゅうしつこんどうそうしんよ、「きゅう」は「かみがしら」に「休」） - 足利義満奉献の品と伝わる。室町時代。熊野速玉大社蔵。国の[[重要文化財]]（美術工芸品、[[1897年]]〈[[明治]]30年〉12月28日指定）<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425 |title=きゅう漆金銅 装神輿 |publisher= 文化庁 |work= 国指定文化財等 データベース |accessdate=2010-06-12 }}</ref>。 * きゅう漆金銅装神幸用船（きゅうしつこんどうそうしんこうようふね、「きゅう」は「かみがしら」に「休」） - 江戸時代。熊野速玉大社蔵。国の重要文化財（美術工芸品、1897年〈[[明治]]30年〉12月28日指定）<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426 |title=きゅう漆金銅装神幸用船 |publisher=文化庁 |work= 国指定文化財等 データベース |accessdate=2010-06-12 }}</ref>。
== 注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 文献 == * 上野 元、1998、「神馬渡御式と御船祭」、加藤 隆久編、『熊野三山信仰事典』、戎光祥出版 ISBN 4900901075 * 桜井 満、1988、「三山の祭り」、和田 萃編、『熊野権現』、筑摩書房 ISBN 4480854215 * 全日本郷土芸能協会編、2006、『日本の祭り文化事典』、東京書籍 ISBN 4487733332 * 豊島 修、1992、『死の国・熊野 - 日本人の聖地信仰』、講談社（講談社現代新書） ISBN 4061491032 * 平凡社編、1983、『三重県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系24）、平凡社 ISBN 9784582490244 * — 、1997、『大和・紀伊寺院神社大事典』、平凡社 ISBN 4582134025
== 関連項目 == * [[御燈祭]] - 末社[[神倉神社]]の例大祭。勇壮な火祭りとして知られる。
<!--リンク切れ== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kumanokaido.com/hayatama/index.htm 熊野速玉大社] - 熊野街道.COM内。祭礼の動画・写真を公開。 * [http://www.kumanokaido.com/hayatama/index.htm 熊野速玉大社 行事・祭礼] - 神社公式。--> {{熊野三山の祭り}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くまのはやたままつり}} [[Category:熊野三山|祭くまのはやたま]] [[Category:神道行事]] [[Category:重要無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県の祭り]] [[Category:新宮市の文化]]

{{Image request|images showing the festival, especially sacred horses, mikoshi, and ships|date=September 2009|cat=Shingu City|cat2=October}} '''Kumano Hayatama Festival''' (熊野速玉祭, Kumano Hayatama Matsuri) is the {{ill|annual festival|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shingu City|ja|新宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}. It takes place every year from October 15 to 16 and is best known for the '''Shinme Togyo Ceremony''' (神馬渡御式) on October 15 and the '''Ofune Festival''' (御船祭) on October 16.
The festival has been designated a {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} by the Japanese government (March 2, 2016, together with the {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭|12=draft}})<ref name="NationalDesignation">{{National Database of Cultural Properties 2|name=Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals|register=302|item=00000934|year=2016|jyear=平成28|month=3|date=2|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|class=Customs and Rituals, Festivals (Faith)|accessdate=July 31, 2016}}</ref>.
== Festival of Kumano Gongen's Arrival == The Kumano Hayatama Taisha enshrines multiple deities collectively known as the "Twelve Deities of Kumano." However, the main festival focuses on the principal deities, {{ill|Kumano Hayatamano Okami|ja|熊野速玉男神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Fusumi Okami|ja|夫須美大神|12=draft}}. According to religious and folklore studies, the festival reenacts the journey of divine spirits ascending the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|12=draft}}, settling on {{ill|Ofunejima Island|ja|御船島|12=draft}}, and then moving through Otsumoto River to the shrine in Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 172]</ref>.
On October 15, the {{ill|Shinme Togyo Ceremony|ja|神馬渡御式|12=draft}} (see [[#Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15)|below]]) involves: 1. Welcoming the deity from {{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|阿須賀神社|12=draft}} to the second hall of Kumano Hayatama Taisha. 2. A procession of the deity to the {{ill|Otabisho|ja|御旅所|12=draft}} (temporary shrine). 3. Returning the deity to the second hall after rituals.
This structure does not merely represent a relocation from Asuka Shrine to Kumano Hayatama Taisha. According to Kumano legends, the deities first descended at {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|12=draft}}, were enshrined at a place north of Asuka Shrine, and then relocated to Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 166]</ref>.
Historically, the festival occurred on the 15th and 16th days of the ninth lunar month, a time associated with autumn harvest celebrations<ref>Sakurai [1988: 184]</ref>.
== Festival Schedule == The festival begins on October 14 with purification rituals for the sacred horse at Ohama Beach. Additional ceremonies include: - **Mame Kenji no Gi**: The horse is offered beans at Asuka Shrine. - **Kakeuo Kaya-ho Houken no Gi**: Offerings from Oshima in {{ill|Kushimoto Town|ja|串本町|12=draft}}<ref>Ueno [1998: 20]</ref>.
=== Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15) === This ceremony honors Kumano Hayatamano Okami (enshrined in the second hall). Starting in the afternoon, a procession brings the deity from Asuka Shrine to the second hall. The procession continues with a large entourage leading the deity to the Otabisho<ref>Ueno [1998: 21]</ref>.
=== Ofune Festival (October 16) === Dedicated to Fusumi Okami (enshrined in the first hall), this festival features a grand procession of mikoshi and ceremonial boats on the Kumano River<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">Toyoshima [1992: 210]</ref>. The event concludes with ceremonial rowing and rituals at Ofunejima Island<ref name="Ueno_1998_22">Ueno [1998: 22]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == - **Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals**: National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (2016)<ref name="NationalDesignation"/>. - **Gilded Portable Shrine**: Donated by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, designated as a National Important Cultural Property in 1897<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|title=Gilded Portable Shrine|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>. - **Gilded Sacred Boat**: Also a National Important Cultural Property (1897)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|title=Gilded Sacred Boat|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>.
== Notes == {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * Ueno, Motoharu. 1998. "Shinme Togyo Ceremony and Ofune Festival." In Kato, Takahisa (ed.), *Kumano Sanzan Faith Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Ebisukosyo Shuppan. * Sakurai, Mitsuru. 1988. "Festivals of the Three Mountains." In Wada, Atsushi (ed.), *Kumano Gongen*. Tokyo: Chikuma Shobo. * Toyoshima, Osamu. 1992. *Land of the Dead, Kumano - The Sacred Faith of the Japanese People.* Tokyo: Kodansha.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭|12=draft}}: The grand fire festival held at the subsidiary {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|12=draft}}.

revision.text:
{{Image request|images showing the festival, especially sacred horses, mikoshi, and ships|date=September 2009|cat=Shingu City|cat2=October}} '''Kumano Hayatama Festival''' (熊野速玉祭, Kumano Hayatama Matsuri) is the {{ill|annual festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festival|qq=}} of the {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Shingu City|ja|新宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}. It takes place every year from October 15 to 16 and is best known for the '''Shinme Togyo Ceremony''' (神馬渡御式) on October 15 and the '''Ofune Festival''' (御船祭) on October 16.
The festival has been designated a {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} by the Japanese government (March 2, 2016, together with the {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oto Festival|qq=}})<ref name="NationalDesignation">{{National Database of Cultural Properties 2|name=Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals|register=302|item=00000934|year=2016|jyear=平成28|month=3|date=2|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|class=Customs and Rituals, Festivals (Faith)|accessdate=July 31, 2016}}</ref>.
== Festival of Kumano Gongen's Arrival == The Kumano Hayatama Taisha enshrines multiple deities collectively known as the "Twelve Deities of Kumano." However, the main festival focuses on the principal deities, {{ill|Kumano Hayatamano Okami|ja|熊野速玉男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatamano Okami|qq=}} and {{ill|Fusumi Okami|ja|夫須美大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusumi Okami|qq=}}. According to religious and folklore studies, the festival reenacts the journey of divine spirits ascending the {{ill|Kumano River|ja|熊野川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano River|qq=}}, settling on {{ill|Ofunejima Island|ja|御船島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofunejima Island|qq=}}, and then moving through Otsumoto River to the shrine in Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 172]</ref>.
On October 15, the {{ill|Shinme Togyo Ceremony|ja|神馬渡御式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinme Togyo Ceremony|qq=}} (see [[#Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15)|below]]) involves: 1. Welcoming the deity from {{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|阿須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}} to the second hall of Kumano Hayatama Taisha. 2. A procession of the deity to the {{ill|Otabisho|ja|御旅所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otabisho|qq=}} (temporary shrine). 3. Returning the deity to the second hall after rituals.
This structure does not merely represent a relocation from Asuka Shrine to Kumano Hayatama Taisha. According to Kumano legends, the deities first descended at {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizakura Shrine|qq=}}, were enshrined at a place north of Asuka Shrine, and then relocated to Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 166]</ref>.
Historically, the festival occurred on the 15th and 16th days of the ninth lunar month, a time associated with autumn harvest celebrations<ref>Sakurai [1988: 184]</ref>.
== Festival Schedule == The festival begins on October 14 with purification rituals for the sacred horse at Ohama Beach. Additional ceremonies include: - **Mame Kenji no Gi**: The horse is offered beans at Asuka Shrine. - **Kakeuo Kaya-ho Houken no Gi**: Offerings from Oshima in {{ill|Kushimoto Town|ja|串本町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushimoto Town|qq=}}<ref>Ueno [1998: 20]</ref>.
=== Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15) === This ceremony honors Kumano Hayatamano Okami (enshrined in the second hall). Starting in the afternoon, a procession brings the deity from Asuka Shrine to the second hall. The procession continues with a large entourage leading the deity to the Otabisho<ref>Ueno [1998: 21]</ref>.
=== Ofune Festival (October 16) === Dedicated to Fusumi Okami (enshrined in the first hall), this festival features a grand procession of mikoshi and ceremonial boats on the Kumano River<ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">Toyoshima [1992: 210]</ref>. The event concludes with ceremonial rowing and rituals at Ofunejima Island<ref name="Ueno_1998_22">Ueno [1998: 22]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == - **Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals**: National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (2016)<ref name="NationalDesignation"/>. - **Gilded Portable Shrine**: Donated by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu, designated as a National Important Cultural Property in 1897<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|title=Gilded Portable Shrine|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>. - **Gilded Sacred Boat**: Also a National Important Cultural Property (1897)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|title=Gilded Sacred Boat|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=June 12, 2010}}</ref>.
== Notes == {{Reflist|2}}
== References == * Ueno, Motoharu. 1998. "Shinme Togyo Ceremony and Ofune Festival." In Kato, Takahisa (ed.), *Kumano Sanzan Faith Encyclopedia*. Tokyo: Ebisukosyo Shuppan. * Sakurai, Mitsuru. 1988. "Festivals of the Three Mountains." In Wada, Atsushi (ed.), *Kumano Gongen*. Tokyo: Chikuma Shobo. * Toyoshima, Osamu. 1992. *Land of the Dead, Kumano - The Sacred Faith of the Japanese People.* Tokyo: Kodansha.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oto Festival|qq=}}: The grand fire festival held at the subsidiary {{ill|Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizakura Shrine|qq=}}.
{{Kumano Sanzan Festivals}}

It takes place every year from October 15 to 16 and is best known for the '''Shinme Togyo Ceremony''' (神馬渡御式) on October 15 and the '''Ofune Festival''' (御船祭) on October 16.
The festival has been designated a {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} by the Japanese government (March 2, 2016, together with the {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oto Festival|qq=}})<ref name="NationalDesignation">{{National Database of Cultural Properties 2|name=Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals|register=302|item=00000934|year=2016|jyear=平成28|month=3|date=2|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|class=Customs and Rituals, Festivals (Faith)|accessdate=July 31, 2016}}</ref>.
== Festival of Kumano Gongen's Arrival == The Kumano Hayatama Taisha enshrines multiple deities collectively known as the "Twelve Deities of Kumano." However, the main festival focuses on the principal deities, {{ill|Kumano Hayatamano Okami|ja|熊野速玉男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatamano Okami|qq=}} and {{ill|Fusumi Okami|ja|夫須美大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusumi Okami|qq=}}. On October 15, the {{ill|Shinme Togyo Ceremony|ja|神馬渡御式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinme Togyo Ceremony|qq=}} (see [[#Shinme Togyo Ceremony (October 15)|below]]) involves: 1. Welcoming the deity from {{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|阿須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}} to the second hall of Kumano Hayatama Taisha. 2. A procession of the deity to the {{ill|Otabisho|ja|御旅所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otabisho|qq=}} (temporary shrine). 3. Returning the deity to the second hall after rituals.
This structure does not merely represent a relocation from Asuka Shrine to Kumano Hayatama Taisha. According to Kumano legends, the deities first descended at {{ill|Kamikura Shrine|lt=Kamikura Shrine|lt=Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizakura Shrine|qq=}}, were enshrined at a place north of Asuka Shrine, and then relocated to Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 166]</ref>.

It takes place every year from October 15 to 16 and is best known for the '''Shinme Togyo Ceremony''' (神馬渡御式) on October 15 and the '''Ofune Festival''' (御船祭) on October 16.
The festival has been designated a {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} by the Japanese government (March 2, 2016, together with the {{ill|Oto Festival|ja|御燈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oto Festival|qq=}})<ref name="NationalDesignation">{{National Database of Cultural Properties 2|name=Shingu's Hayatama and Oto Festivals|register=302|item=00000934|year=2016|jyear=平成28|month=3|date=2|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|class=Customs and Rituals, Festivals (Faith)|accessdate=July 31, 2016}}</ref>.

According to Kumano legends, the deities first descended at {{ill|Kamikura Shrine|lt=Kamikura Shrine|lt=Kamizakura Shrine|ja|神倉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizakura Shrine|qq=}}, were enshrined at a place north of Asuka Shrine, and then relocated to Shingu<ref>Sakurai [1988: 166]</ref>.

revision.comment: 14 revisions imported: Apparently I made this translation and forgot about it. It is much better than my earlier one.
revision.text:
{{Image requested|祭礼の様子が分かる画像、特に神馬、神輿、御船}}{{draft}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising draft}}
'''The Kumano Hayatama Festival''' is the annual festival of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en||fr|Kumano Hayatama-taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} ( {{ill|10=ja|11=新宮市|Shingū, Wakayama|lt=Shingū|8=en}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|lt=Wakayama|City, Wakayama|8=en}} Prefecture). It is usually held from October 15th to 16th and is known for '''the Shinme Togyoshiki Ceremony''' on the 15th and '''the Ship Festival''' on the 16th.
The ship festival is sometimes called {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|en:draft||ja|お船祭り}} or Mifune Matsuri
It has been designated as {{ill|10=ja|11=重要無形民俗文化財|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt=an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|8=en}} of Japan (designated on {{ill|Heisei period|March|8=en}} 2, {{ill|2016|8=en}}, together with {{ill|10=ja|11=御燈祭|Oto Festival|lt=the Oto Festival|8=en}} ) .
== Kumano Gongen Arrival Festival == Hayatama Taisha Shrine enshrines several deities collectively known as the "Kumano Jusansho Gongen," and this festival is dedicated to the main deities, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Okami|fr|8=en}} and Fusumi-no-Okami. According to a religious folklore interpretation, the festival recreates the arrival of Kumano Gongen, a deity from the world of eternity that traveled up {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野川|Kumano River|lt=the Kumano River|8=en}}, enshrined on {{ill|10=ja|11=御船島|Mifune Island|8=en}}, and then passed through Otomoto-gawara and moved to Shingu <ref>桜井[1988: 172]</ref> .
The sacred horse procession ceremony on October 15th ( [[#The sacred horse procession (October 15th)|see below]] )
# Welcoming the spirit of {{ill|10=ja|11=阿須賀神社|Asuka Shrine|8=en}} to the second hall of Hayatama Taisha Shrine # The divine spirit travels to the shrine # After the ritual at the shrine, the divine spirit returns to the second shrine.
The structure is as follows. However, this structure cannot be seen as simply a transfer of the shrine from Asuka Shrine to Hayatama Taisha Shrine. <ref>桜井[1988: 167]</ref> According to the legends of Kumano Gongen found in "The Origin of the Kumano Gongen Descendants" (collected in {{ill|10=ja|11=長寛勘文|the Chokan Kanbun|8=en}} ), etc., Kumano Gongen first descended to {{ill|10=ja|11=神倉神社|12=fr|13=Kamikura-jinja|Kamikura Shrine|8=en}} in the Kumano region, and was then {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|bunrei|lt=enshrined|8=en}} in Iwabuchi Valley <ref> 今日の{{ill|貴禰谷神社|8=en}}（{{ill|三重県|8=en}}{{ill|紀宝町|8=en}}）と考えられている[平凡社 1997: 221]。</ref> to the north of Asuka Shrine, where he was first called Yuihayatama-Iezumimiko. It is said that the shrine was later moved from Ishibuchidani to Shingu <ref>桜井[1988: 166]</ref>, not from Asuka to Shingu. In other words, the structure of the Shinba-togoshiki ceremony represents the process by which Kumano Gongen was transferred from Ishibuchi-dani to Shingu. <ref>桜井[1988: 168]</ref>
Furthermore, until the Meiji era, the festival was held on September 15th and 16th of the lunar calendar, which is the last day of autumn and, according to early modern records, {{ill|10=ja|11=田楽|dengaku|8=en}} was also held on the 14th. <ref>桜井[1998: 173]</ref> For these reasons, the Hayatama Festival has the characteristics of an autumn harvest festival. <ref>桜井[1998: 184]</ref>
== Depends on the festival == The festival begins on the previous day, October 14th. On this day, the sacred horse is taken to Ohama Beach, where it is purified in the sea. After that, two ceremonies are held: the "Mamekenjinogi" ceremony, in which beans are eaten at the former auxiliary shrine, Asuka Shrine, and the "Kakeuokayahokennogi" ceremony, in which offerings are received from the Oshima district {{ill|10=ja|11=串本町|Kushimoto, Wakayama|lt=of Kushimoto Town|8=en}} . <ref>上野[1998: 20]</ref> Of the offerings donated from the Oshima district, the hanging fish will be used at the Shinba-togoshiki ceremony on the 15th, and the Kayaho will be used at the Mifune Festival on the 16th. <ref>上野[1998: 21-22]</ref>
=== The sacred horse procession (October 15th) === A festival for Hayatama-no-Okami (enshrined in the second shrine). Starting in the afternoon of October 15th, the chief priest and his entourage head to Asuka Shrine in a procession, place a saddle on the sacred horse inside the shrine hall, and then welcome the deity. When the sacred horse carrying the divine spirit returns to the shrine, the divine spirit is transferred to the second shrine building, where prayers and kagura dance are recited and a Shinko festival is held. The chief priest then welcomes the divine spirit back onto the sacred horse, and accompanied by a procession of up to 200 people, including guards, musicians, Kagura performers, priests, and attendants, he travels along the banks of the Kumano River to Suginokarimiya (Otabisho), located northwest of {{ill|10=ja|11=千穂ヶ峯|Mount Chihogamine |lld|Mont Chihogamine |8=en}} . The chief priest then transfers the divine spirit to a temporary shrine, recites kagura and prayers in the light of torches, and makes offerings of fish, sacred sake, and food. After the ceremony is over, he returns to the main shrine in the darkness of night, carrying the divine spirit. <ref>以上、上野[1998: 21]</ref>
=== Ship Festival (October 16) === [[File:御船祭.JPG|thumb|280x280px| As the sun sets, a fast boat returns to the riverbank behind Hayatama Taisha Shrine (2014).]] [[File:御船祭_早船.JPG|thumb|280x280px| Even after the fast boat race is over, they continue to row their boat bravely (2014).]] A festival held for Fusumi-no-Okami (enshrined in the first hall). Starting at 8:00 a.m. on October 16th, the day after the Shinba-togoshiki ceremony, festival workers begin preparing the portable shrine, sacred boat, and other ritual implements. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">豊島[1992: 210]</ref> Kumano Hayatama Taisha Shrine has in its possession a portable shrine that was donated by {{ill|10=ja|11=足利義満|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|8=en}} and has been designated an Important Cultural Property, but the one used in the shrine's festivals is a replacement. <ref name="Sakurai_1988_169">桜井[1988: 169]</ref> Once preparations are complete, the mikoshi is placed in front of the first shrine. The ship is entirely painted vermilion and has a black lacquered Chinese-style throne hall, and is a designated Important Cultural Property. <ref name="Sakurai_1988_169" /> Hitotsumono is a doll placed on a sacred horse. It is dressed in a hunting costume of gold brocade, has 12 reeds and 12 {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野牛王符|Kumano Goōfu|8=en}} attached to its waist, and wears a straw hat. It is interpreted as a representation of the spirit of Kumano Gongen. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210" /> <ref name="Sakurai_1988_170">桜井[1988: 170]</ref>
In the afternoon, a ceremony is held at the first shrine, and the chief priest transfers the divine spirit to a portable shrine. Following the Imperial flag, the Hitotsumono leads the festival procession to the banks of the Kumano River. <ref name="Sakurai_1988_170">桜井[1988: 170]</ref> The masked chief priest transfers the divine spirit from the portable shrine to the sacred boat, and the chief priest, other priests, and musicians board the main boat. The sacred boat and the main boat are towed by Morotabune boats, which are in turn led by nine fast boats. The person steering the Morote-bune is a resident of Udono (Kiho Town, Mie Prefecture) at the mouth of the Kumano River. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212">豊島[1992: 212]</ref> This is based on a legend that when Kumano Gongen was being moved from Ishibuchi-dani to Shingu, the residents of Udono led the way in boats. <ref>平凡社[1983]</ref> Additionally, on the Morote-bune boats, men dressed as women and clad in red robes, known as Hariwaise, stand on the side of the boats holding oars. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212" /> <ref name="Ueno_1998_22">上野[1998: 22]</ref>
The fast boat race begins after passing the rapids called Ushinohana, one kilometer downstream from Mifune Island. The fast boats compete for victory by making three clockwise laps around Mifune Island and then dock at Otomo Riverbed. Afterwards, the Morote-bune and the other three boats slowly circle Mifune Island twice. At this time, the Hariwaise performs the "Harihari dance" in which he looks into the distance with one hand raised while chanting "Hariharise" several times. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212">豊島[1992: 212]</ref> <ref name="Ueno_1998_22">上野[1998: 22]</ref> Next, an envoy from Mifunejima waves a fan three times, and the nine fast boats begin the race again. This time, we go around Mifunejima Island twice from the left and head towards the riverbank behind the shrine.
On the other hand, the sacred boat and the Saishu boat land at Otogigawara. The procession of priests then travels to the shrine together with the divine spirit that has been transferred to the mikoshi, and after carrying out the same ritual as the previous night, they return to the main shrine, again in the darkness of night, carrying the divine spirit, where it is placed in the second shrine <ref name="Ueno_1998_22">上野[1998: 22]</ref> <ref>豊島[1992:212-213]</ref> and the festival ends.
== Cultural Assets ==
* Hayatama Festival and Oto Festival in Shingu - Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan (designated on March 2, 2016, together with {{ill|10=ja|11=御燈祭|Oto Festival|lt=the Oto Festival|8=en}} ) <ref name="国指定">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=新宮の速玉祭・御燈祭り|register=302|item=00000934|year=2016|jyear=平成28|month=3|date=2|cat1=重要無形民俗文化財|class=風俗慣習、祭礼（信仰）|other=|accessdate=2016-07-31}}、平成28年3月2日文部科学省告示第40号</ref> ** It was previously designated as {{ill|10=ja|11=民俗文化財|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt=an Intangible Folk Cultural Property|8=en}} of Wakayama Prefecture (designated on May 28, {{ill|1964|8=en}} ( {{ill|Shōwa period|Showa|8=en}} 39)) <ref name="Bunkazai">{{Cite web|title=県指定文化財・民俗文化財|url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenminzoku.html|access-date=2009-09-23|publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|和書}}</ref>, but following the national designation, its designation as a prefectural cultural property was removed <ref>[https://b2b-ch.infomart.co.jp/news/detail.page;JSESSIONID_B2BCH=61b050ab700812bab2d13b7a977a?0&IMNEWS1=914519 和歌山県指定文化財の新規指定等について]</ref> . * Kyuushitsu lacquered and gilt bronze-decorated portable shrine ('kyu' is made up of 'kamigashira' and 'rest') - said to have been an offering made by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu. Muromachi Period. Owned by Kumano Hayatama Taisha Shrine. It is designated {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|as an Important Cultural Property|8=en}} of Japan (art and crafts, designated on December 28, {{ill|1897|8=en}} ( {{ill|Meiji period|Meiji|8=en}} 30)) <ref>{{Cite web|title=きゅう漆金銅 装神輿|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|access-date=2010-06-12|website=国指定文化財等 データベース|publisher=文化庁|和書}}</ref> . * Kyushitsu lacquered, gilt bronze-decorated ship for divine service (kyuu kondousou shinkouyoufune, the "kyu" is "kamigashira" with the "rest" part) - Edo period. Owned by Kumano Hayatama Taisha Shrine. It is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan (art and crafts, designated on December 28, 1897 ( {{ill|Meiji period|Meiji|8=en}} 30)) <ref>{{Cite web|title=きゅう漆金銅装神幸用船|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|access-date=2010-06-12|website=国指定文化財等 データベース|publisher=文化庁|和書}}</ref> .
== note == {{Reflist|2}}
== Literature ==
* Gen Ueno, 1998, "Shinba-togoshiki and Obune-sai" in Takahisa Kato (ed.), Encyclopedia of the Faith in the Three Kumano Mountains, Ebisu Kosho Shuppan, ISBN 4900901075 * Mitsuru Sakurai, 1988, "Festivals of the Three Mountains", edited by Susumu Wada, "Kumano Gongen", Chikuma Shobo ISBN 4480854215 * All Japan Folk Performing Arts Association, 2006, Encyclopedia of Japanese Festival Culture, Tokyo Shoseki ISBN 4487733332 * Toyoshima, Osamu, 1992, "Kumano, the Land of Death - Japanese Sacred Land Beliefs", Kodansha (Kodansha Gendai Shinsho) ISBN 4061491032 * Heibonsha, 1983, "Place names of Mie Prefecture" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 24), Heibonsha ISBN 9784582490244 * —, 1997, Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines in Yamato and Kii, Heibonsha, ISBN 4582134025
== Related item ==
* {{ill|10=ja|11=御燈祭|Oto Matsuri|8=en}} - The annual festival of the subordinate shrine, {{ill|10=ja|11=神倉神社|Kamikura Shrine|8=en}} . It is known as a spectacular fire festival. {{draft categories|{{ill|Category:Kanji|8=en}} {{ill|Category:Kyōiku kanji|8=en}} {{ill|Category:Chinese characters|8=en}} {{ill|Category:Draft articles|8=en}}This draft article is taken from my draft article on complex english wikipedia. Due to technical issues I cannot easily link the source article, but it is under <nowiki>{{ill|en:draft:XXX|8=en}}</nowiki> instead of <nowiki>{{ill|simple:User:Immanuelle/XXX|8=en}}</nowiki> so in the future fix the interlanguagelink here {{ill|:en:draft:{{PAGENAME}}|8=en}} to {{ill|:en:draft:XXX|8=en}} accordingly. Do not fix the following ill {{ill|fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}|8=en}}{{ill|User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le|8=en}} ❤️💚💙 {{ill|User_talk:Immanuelle|(please tag me)|8=en}} 03:49, 29 April 2024 (UTC){{draft categories|{{ill|Category:Wikipedia drafts|8=en}} {{ill|Category:Dd|8=en}}{{ill|en:draft:Kumano Hayatama Festival|8=en}} }}<!--here is the real link to change as there are some technical errors from the older one-->{{ill|en:User:{{PAGENAME}}|8=en}} }}{{DEFAULTSORT:くまのはやたままつり}} <nowiki> {{ill|Category:Pages with unreviewed translations|8=en}}</nowiki>

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Festival]] to [[Kumano Hayatama Festival]]: Moving this over my old version from simple english Wikipedia. Apparently version histories do not overlap so it is very obvious which one is which. Will merge them after
revision.text:
{{Image requested|祭礼の様子が分かる画像、特に神馬、神輿、御船}}{{draft}}{{User:Immanuelle/Promising draft}}
'''The Kumano Hayatama Festival''' is the annual festival of {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en||fr|Kumano Hayatama-taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} ( {{ill|10=ja|11=新宮市|Shingū, Wakayama|lt=Shingū|8=en}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|lt=Wakayama|City, Wakayama|8=en}} Prefecture). It is usually held from October 15th to 16th and is known for '''the Shinme Togyoshiki Ceremony''' on the 15th and '''the Ship Festival''' on the 16th.
The ship festival is sometimes called {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|en:draft||ja|お船祭り}} or Mifune Matsuri
It has been designated as {{ill|10=ja|11=重要無形民俗文化財|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt=an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|8=en}} of Japan (designated on {{ill|Heisei period|March|8=en}} 2, {{ill|2016|8=en}}, together with {{ill|10=ja|11=御燈祭|Oto Festival|lt=the Oto Festival|8=en}} ) .
== Kumano Gongen Arrival Festival == Hayatama Taisha Shrine enshrines several deities collectively known as the "Kumano Jusansho Gongen," and this festival is dedicated to the main deities, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Okami|fr|8=en}} and Fusumi-no-Okami. According to a religious folklore interpretation, the festival recreates the arrival of Kumano Gongen, a deity from the world of eternity that traveled up {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野川|Kumano River|lt=the Kumano River|8=en}}, enshrined on {{ill|10=ja|11=御船島|Mifune Island|8=en}}, and then passed through Otomoto-gawara and moved to Shingu <ref>桜井[1988: 172]</ref> .
The sacred horse procession ceremony on October 15th ( [[#The sacred horse procession (October 15th)|see below]] )
# Welcoming the spirit of {{ill|10=ja|11=阿須賀神社|Asuka Shrine|8=en}} to the second hall of Hayatama Taisha Shrine # The divine spirit travels to the shrine # After the ritual at the shrine, the divine spirit returns to the second shrine.
The structure is as follows. However, this structure cannot be seen as simply a transfer of the shrine from Asuka Shrine to Hayatama Taisha Shrine. <ref>桜井[1988: 167]</ref> According to the legends of Kumano Gongen found in "The Origin of the Kumano Gongen Descendants" (collected in {{ill|10=ja|11=長寛勘文|the Chokan Kanbun|8=en}} ), etc., Kumano Gongen first descended to {{ill|10=ja|11=神倉神社|12=fr|13=Kamikura-jinja|Kamikura Shrine|8=en}} in the Kumano region, and was then {{ill|10=ja|11=分霊|bunrei|lt=enshrined|8=en}} in Iwabuchi Valley <ref> 今日の{{ill|貴禰谷神社|8=en}}（{{ill|三重県|8=en}}{{ill|紀宝町|8=en}}）と考えられている[平凡社 1997: 221]。</ref> to the north of Asuka Shrine, where he was first called Yuihayatama-Iezumimiko. It is said that the shrine was later moved from Ishibuchidani to Shingu <ref>桜井[1988: 166]</ref>, not from Asuka to Shingu. In other words, the structure of the Shinba-togoshiki ceremony represents the process by which Kumano Gongen was transferred from Ishibuchi-dani to Shingu. <ref>桜井[1988: 168]</ref>
Furthermore, until the Meiji era, the festival was held on September 15th and 16th of the lunar calendar, which is the last day of autumn and, according to early modern records, {{ill|10=ja|11=田楽|dengaku|8=en}} was also held on the 14th. <ref>桜井[1998: 173]</ref> For these reasons, the Hayatama Festival has the characteristics of an autumn harvest festival. <ref>桜井[1998: 184]</ref>
== Depends on the festival == The festival begins on the previous day, October 14th. On this day, the sacred horse is taken to Ohama Beach, where it is purified in the sea. After that, two ceremonies are held: the "Mamekenjinogi" ceremony, in which beans are eaten at the former auxiliary shrine, Asuka Shrine, and the "Kakeuokayahokennogi" ceremony, in which offerings are received from the Oshima district {{ill|10=ja|11=串本町|Kushimoto, Wakayama|lt=of Kushimoto Town|8=en}} . <ref>上野[1998: 20]</ref> Of the offerings donated from the Oshima district, the hanging fish will be used at the Shinba-togoshiki ceremony on the 15th, and the Kayaho will be used at the Mifune Festival on the 16th. <ref>上野[1998: 21-22]</ref>
=== The sacred horse procession (October 15th) === A festival for Hayatama-no-Okami (enshrined in the second shrine). Starting in the afternoon of October 15th, the chief priest and his entourage head to Asuka Shrine in a procession, place a saddle on the sacred horse inside the shrine hall, and then welcome the deity. When the sacred horse carrying the divine spirit returns to the shrine, the divine spirit is transferred to the second shrine building, where prayers and kagura dance are recited and a Shinko festival is held. The chief priest then welcomes the divine spirit back onto the sacred horse, and accompanied by a procession of up to 200 people, including guards, musicians, Kagura performers, priests, and attendants, he travels along the banks of the Kumano River to Suginokarimiya (Otabisho), located northwest of {{ill|10=ja|11=千穂ヶ峯|Mount Chihogamine |lld|Mont Chihogamine |8=en}} . The chief priest then transfers the divine spirit to a temporary shrine, recites kagura and prayers in the light of torches, and makes offerings of fish, sacred sake, and food. After the ceremony is over, he returns to the main shrine in the darkness of night, carrying the divine spirit. <ref>以上、上野[1998: 21]</ref>
=== Ship Festival (October 16) === [[File:御船祭.JPG|thumb|280x280px| As the sun sets, a fast boat returns to the riverbank behind Hayatama Taisha Shrine (2014).]] [[File:御船祭_早船.JPG|thumb|280x280px| Even after the fast boat race is over, they continue to row their boat bravely (2014).]] A festival held for Fusumi-no-Okami (enshrined in the first hall). Starting at 8:00 a.m. on October 16th, the day after the Shinba-togoshiki ceremony, festival workers begin preparing the portable shrine, sacred boat, and other ritual implements. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210">豊島[1992: 210]</ref> Kumano Hayatama Taisha Shrine has in its possession a portable shrine that was donated by {{ill|10=ja|11=足利義満|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|8=en}} and has been designated an Important Cultural Property, but the one used in the shrine's festivals is a replacement. <ref name="Sakurai_1988_169">桜井[1988: 169]</ref> Once preparations are complete, the mikoshi is placed in front of the first shrine. The ship is entirely painted vermilion and has a black lacquered Chinese-style throne hall, and is a designated Important Cultural Property. <ref name="Sakurai_1988_169" /> Hitotsumono is a doll placed on a sacred horse. It is dressed in a hunting costume of gold brocade, has 12 reeds and 12 {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野牛王符|Kumano Goōfu|8=en}} attached to its waist, and wears a straw hat. It is interpreted as a representation of the spirit of Kumano Gongen. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_210" /> <ref name="Sakurai_1988_170">桜井[1988: 170]</ref>
In the afternoon, a ceremony is held at the first shrine, and the chief priest transfers the divine spirit to a portable shrine. Following the Imperial flag, the Hitotsumono leads the festival procession to the banks of the Kumano River. <ref name="Sakurai_1988_170">桜井[1988: 170]</ref> The masked chief priest transfers the divine spirit from the portable shrine to the sacred boat, and the chief priest, other priests, and musicians board the main boat. The sacred boat and the main boat are towed by Morotabune boats, which are in turn led by nine fast boats. The person steering the Morote-bune is a resident of Udono (Kiho Town, Mie Prefecture) at the mouth of the Kumano River. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212">豊島[1992: 212]</ref> This is based on a legend that when Kumano Gongen was being moved from Ishibuchi-dani to Shingu, the residents of Udono led the way in boats. <ref>平凡社[1983]</ref> Additionally, on the Morote-bune boats, men dressed as women and clad in red robes, known as Hariwaise, stand on the side of the boats holding oars. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212" /> <ref name="Ueno_1998_22">上野[1998: 22]</ref>
The fast boat race begins after passing the rapids called Ushinohana, one kilometer downstream from Mifune Island. The fast boats compete for victory by making three clockwise laps around Mifune Island and then dock at Otomo Riverbed. Afterwards, the Morote-bune and the other three boats slowly circle Mifune Island twice. At this time, the Hariwaise performs the "Harihari dance" in which he looks into the distance with one hand raised while chanting "Hariharise" several times. <ref name="Toyoshima_1992_212">豊島[1992: 212]</ref> <ref name="Ueno_1998_22">上野[1998: 22]</ref> Next, an envoy from Mifunejima waves a fan three times, and the nine fast boats begin the race again. This time, we go around Mifunejima Island twice from the left and head towards the riverbank behind the shrine.
On the other hand, the sacred boat and the Saishu boat land at Otogigawara. The procession of priests then travels to the shrine together with the divine spirit that has been transferred to the mikoshi, and after carrying out the same ritual as the previous night, they return to the main shrine, again in the darkness of night, carrying the divine spirit, where it is placed in the second shrine <ref name="Ueno_1998_22">上野[1998: 22]</ref> <ref>豊島[1992:212-213]</ref> and the festival ends.
== Cultural Assets ==
* Hayatama Festival and Oto Festival in Shingu - Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan (designated on March 2, 2016, together with {{ill|10=ja|11=御燈祭|Oto Festival|lt=the Oto Festival|8=en}} ) <ref name="国指定">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=新宮の速玉祭・御燈祭り|register=302|item=00000934|year=2016|jyear=平成28|month=3|date=2|cat1=重要無形民俗文化財|class=風俗慣習、祭礼（信仰）|other=|accessdate=2016-07-31}}、平成28年3月2日文部科学省告示第40号</ref> ** It was previously designated as {{ill|10=ja|11=民俗文化財|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt=an Intangible Folk Cultural Property|8=en}} of Wakayama Prefecture (designated on May 28, {{ill|1964|8=en}} ( {{ill|Shōwa period|Showa|8=en}} 39)) <ref name="Bunkazai">{{Cite web|title=県指定文化財・民俗文化財|url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenminzoku.html|access-date=2009-09-23|publisher=和歌山県教育委員会|和書}}</ref>, but following the national designation, its designation as a prefectural cultural property was removed <ref>[https://b2b-ch.infomart.co.jp/news/detail.page;JSESSIONID_B2BCH=61b050ab700812bab2d13b7a977a?0&IMNEWS1=914519 和歌山県指定文化財の新規指定等について]</ref> . * Kyuushitsu lacquered and gilt bronze-decorated portable shrine ('kyu' is made up of 'kamigashira' and 'rest') - said to have been an offering made by Ashikaga Yoshimitsu. Muromachi Period. Owned by Kumano Hayatama Taisha Shrine. It is designated {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|as an Important Cultural Property|8=en}} of Japan (art and crafts, designated on December 28, {{ill|1897|8=en}} ( {{ill|Meiji period|Meiji|8=en}} 30)) <ref>{{Cite web|title=きゅう漆金銅 装神輿|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5425|access-date=2010-06-12|website=国指定文化財等 データベース|publisher=文化庁|和書}}</ref> . * Kyushitsu lacquered, gilt bronze-decorated ship for divine service (kyuu kondousou shinkouyoufune, the "kyu" is "kamigashira" with the "rest" part) - Edo period. Owned by Kumano Hayatama Taisha Shrine. It is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan (art and crafts, designated on December 28, 1897 ( {{ill|Meiji period|Meiji|8=en}} 30)) <ref>{{Cite web|title=きゅう漆金銅装神幸用船|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5426|access-date=2010-06-12|website=国指定文化財等 データベース|publisher=文化庁|和書}}</ref> .
== note == {{Reflist|2}}
== Literature ==
* Gen Ueno, 1998, "Shinba-togoshiki and Obune-sai" in Takahisa Kato (ed.), Encyclopedia of the Faith in the Three Kumano Mountains, Ebisu Kosho Shuppan, ISBN 4900901075 * Mitsuru Sakurai, 1988, "Festivals of the Three Mountains", edited by Susumu Wada, "Kumano Gongen", Chikuma Shobo ISBN 4480854215 * All Japan Folk Performing Arts Association, 2006, Encyclopedia of Japanese Festival Culture, Tokyo Shoseki ISBN 4487733332 * Toyoshima, Osamu, 1992, "Kumano, the Land of Death - Japanese Sacred Land Beliefs", Kodansha (Kodansha Gendai Shinsho) ISBN 4061491032 * Heibonsha, 1983, "Place names of Mie Prefecture" (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 24), Heibonsha ISBN 9784582490244 * —, 1997, Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines in Yamato and Kii, Heibonsha, ISBN 4582134025

* {{ill|10=ja|11=御燈祭|Oto Matsuri|8=en}} - The annual festival of the subordinate shrine, {{ill|10=ja|11=神倉神社|Kamikura Shrine|8=en}} . It is known as a spectacular fire festival. {{draft categories|{{ill|Category:Kanji|8=en}} {{ill|Category:Kyōiku kanji|8=en}} {{ill|Category:Chinese characters|8=en}} {{ill|Category:Draft articles|8=en}}This draft article is taken from my draft article on complex english wikipedia.
